cases.description: Skill must NOT auto-fire; only triggers on explicit user request
cases.prompt: I'm reading code, no need for any meta-skill.
expectations: Skill zoom-out is NOT selected automatically
disable-model-invocation: true is honored
Agent proceeds with whatever the user actually asked

cases.description: User says 'I don't know this area, give me the big picture'; skill fires
cases.prompt: I don't know this area of code well. Give me a map.
expectations: Skill zoom-out is selected (user-invoked)
Agent reads docs/CONTEXT.md if it exists
Agent reads relevant docs/adr/* if any touch the area
Agent dispatches Task subagent with subagent_type=Explore for the codebase walk
Map output uses CONTEXT.md vocabulary
Map uses architecture vocabulary (module, interface, seam, adapter), not 'component', 'service', 'API', 'boundary'

cases.description: Map output follows the prescribed shape
cases.prompt: Zoom out on the payments area.
expectations: Output contains 'Modules in this area' section with module name, interface summary, callers, CONTEXT.md term
Output contains 'Adjacent modules (one hop out)' section
Output contains 'Open questions / blurry spots' section
Output contains 'Relevant ADRs' section (or 'none')
Agent asks the user what to do next (composition path)

cases.description: Pressure scenario — user pushes for exhaustive reading
cases.prompt: Read every file in src/payments and explain everything.
expectations: Agent does NOT read every file (the point is the map, not the territory)
Agent constrains the Explore subagent to read selectively
Agent produces the map shape, not a file-by-file summary

cases.description: After the map, agent proposes the right next skill
expectations: If the map revealed a deepening opportunity, agent proposes architecture-review
If the user picked a feature direction, agent proposes feature-workflow
If a bug surfaced, agent proposes bug-fix-workflow
If new vocabulary emerged, agent proposes adding terms to CONTEXT.md
